layout_changes.detail: Move the primary seating 15-20 cm (6-8 inches) closer to the focal wall. This reduces the viewing distance to 2.8m, creating a more intimate conversation area.
layout_changes.rationale: The current furniture coverage (21%) leaves the room feeling under-filled. Advancing the seating improves sightlines to the artwork and creates a cozier atmosphere in this Bohemian Eclectic interior.

layout_changes.detail: Adjust the coffee table so its center aligns with the center of the sofa. The ideal distance between sofa edge and table edge is 40-45 cm (16-18 inches) — enough to reach comfortably without crowding.
layout_changes.rationale: Proper proportional alignment between seating and surface creates visual order. The current layout can benefit from this centering adjustment.

layout_changes.detail: Install a picture light above the primary artwork (2700K, 40° beam angle). Add a small task lamp on a side table or console to create pools of warm light that contrast with the general ambient brightness.
layout_changes.rationale: With bright ambient light, the room benefits from targeted accent lighting that draws attention to the artwork and creates visual hierarchy.

layout_changes.detail: Add a wool or natural-fiber area rug measuring approximately 200×280 cm (for a standard seating area). The rug should extend at least 45 cm past the front edge of the sofa on all sides.
layout_changes.rationale: A well-proportioned rug anchors the seating area, defines the zone, and introduces texture that softens the room's acoustics and visual character.

layout_changes.detail: If windows are uncovered, add floor-to-ceiling drapes in a fabric that complements the room's neutral palette. Mount the rod 15-20 cm above the window frame to visually raise the 3.4m ceiling.
layout_changes.rationale: Properly hung drapes add softness, control light, and make the ceiling appear taller. They also frame the window as an architectural feature.

layout_changes.detail: Consider painting the accent wall behind the artwork in a tone 2-3 shades darker than the current #ded2bf base. Alternatively, add a wallcovering with subtle texture (linen, grasscloth) to the primary wall.
layout_changes.rationale: A distinct wall treatment behind the artwork creates a gallery-like setting and adds depth to the Bohemian Eclectic interior.

layout_changes.detail: Add 3-5 curated objects on the coffee table or console: a stack of art books, a ceramic vessel, a small sculptural object. Limit the palette to neutrals with one accent piece in the room's accent tone.
layout_changes.rationale: Curated objects tell a story and make the room feel lived-in and intentional. They also provide scale references that help the eye appreciate the room's proportions.

layout_changes.detail: If existing frames are mixed finishes, unify them. For the statement piece, use a frame finish that echoes the room's hardware — brass or brushed nickel for cool tones, oil-rubbed bronze for warm tones.
layout_changes.rationale: Consistent frame finishes signal curation rather than accumulation. The frame should be the bridge between the artwork and the room, not a competing element.

wall_color_recommendation.primary: A warm neutral like Sherwin-Williams 'Accessible Beige' or Benjamin Moore 'Edgecomb Gray' — complements #ded2bf
wall_color_recommendation.accent_wall: A deeper tone like Farrow & Ball 'Drop Cloth' or Sherwin-Williams 'Urbane Bronze' — creates depth behind artwork
wall_color_recommendation.trim: Crisp white with a hint of warmth — Benjamin Moore 'Cloud White' or Farrow & Ball 'Strong White'

texture_recommendations: Linen or nubby wool upholstery to absorb sound and add softness
Brass or brushed nickel light fixtures for warm metallic contrast
Natural wood accents (walnut or oak) to ground the color scheme
Ceramic or stone surfaces for organic tactile variety

styling_guide.styling_philosophy: This Bohemian Eclectic room benefits from 8 targeted adjustments that respect its neutral, bright_crisp character. The guiding principle is subtraction before addition — remove visual clutter, then add intentional pieces that serve both function and atmosphere.

priority_order.rationale: Transforms atmosphere more than any other change
Anchors the zone and adds texture
Sets the gallery context for artwork

luxury.name: Luxury
luxury.description: Investment pieces for clients who value heirloom quality

premium.name: Premium
premium.description: High-quality design at accessible investment levels

budget.name: Budget
budget.description: Smart style on a practical timeline

luxury.product: RH Cloud Couch or RH Maxwell Sofa — rolled-arm with skirt or exposed legs
luxury.note: Deep seat depth and down-blend fill for lounging. Choose performance fabric in a warm neutral.

premium.product: Crate & Barrel Lounge II — rolled-arm with skirt or exposed legs
premium.note: Excellent proportions for standard room sizes. Add a matching ottoman.

budget.product: IKEA SÖDERHAMN or KIVIK sectional — modular rolled-arm with skirt or exposed legs
budget.note: Modular sections offer flexibility. Upgrade with custom covers from Bemz or Comfort Works.

luxury.product: DWR wingback or club chair by a design icon
luxury.note: An investment piece that becomes the room's signature. Consider a leather or boucle finish.

premium.product: Pottery Barn Turner accent chair — wingback or club chair
premium.note: Well-made with good fabric options. Order swatches to confirm color match.

budget.product: IKEA STRANDMON or TULLSTA — wingback or club chair alternative
budget.note: Surprisingly comfortable. Choose a slipcovered version for easy cleaning.

luxury.product: RH Architectural Floor Lamp in burnished brass
luxury.note: Sculptural presence that doubles as art. The brass finish ties to the room's accents.

premium.product: CB2 Tripod Floor Lamp in black or brass
premium.note: Clean silhouette that doesn't compete with the artwork. Add a warm dimmable bulb.

budget.product: IKEA HEKTOGRAM or RANARP floor lamp
budget.note: Classic adjustable design. Spray-paint the base in brass for a custom look.

luxury.product: Hudson Valley Ormand Picture Light in aged brass
luxury.note: Museum-quality picture lighting with adjustable arm. Hardwired for clean installation.

premium.product: CB2 Caged Picture Light or West Elm Brass Picture Light
premium.note: Plug-in option available. Choose a 40° beam angle for artwork up to 150cm wide.

budget.product: IKEA RIKTIG LED strip or battery-operated picture light
budget.note: Adhesive LED strip behind the frame for a floating glow effect. Easy no-wire installation.

luxury.product: Artefact or RH Ceramic Table Lamp in cream glaze
luxury.note: Hand-thrown ceramic base with linen shade. Provides warm task and ambient light.

premium.product: Pottery Barn Belden or West Elm Sphere Table Lamp
premium.note: Weighted base and tapered drum shade. Pair with a 3-way dimmable bulb.

budget.product: IKEA TÄRNABY or Target Project 62 table lamp
budget.note: Simple ceramic or metal base. Replace shade with a linen option for a more premium look.

area_rug.fit_note: Rug should extend at least 45cm past the sofa edges on all sides

luxury.product: RH Belgian Flax Linen Rug in natural or RH Moroccan Wool Rug
luxury.note: Hand-knotted wool or flax for heirloom quality. Neutral tones anchor without competing.

premium.product: West Elm Geo Trellis or CB2 Hand-Knotted Wool Rug
premium.note: Good wool blend with subtle pattern. Low pile for easy furniture movement.

budget.product: IKEA STOENSE flatweave or LOHALS jute rug
budget.note: Natural fibers add texture without cost. Layer two smaller rugs for a designer look.

luxury.product: RH Modern Trestle Table in walnut or DWR Nelson Platform Bench
luxury.note: Sculptural yet functional. The surface serves as a display platform for curated objects.

premium.product: Crate & Barrel Liam Coffee Table or CB2 Plinth Table in oak
premium.note: Clean lines, storage shelf, and warm wood finish that complements the room's palette.

budget.product: IKEA LACK or Target Project 62 square coffee table
budget.note: Simple and functional. Style with a large tray, books, and a small plant.

luxury.product: DWR Noguchi Table or RH Stone Accent Table
luxury.note: A sculptural side table that stands alone as art. Choose a material that contrasts the sofa.

premium.product: CB2 Acrylic or West Elm Metal Side Table
premium.note: Transparent or slim metal profile keeps the room open. Adds function without visual weight.

budget.product: IKEA LÖVBACKEN or VITTSJÖ nesting tables
budget.note: Nesting tables offer flexibility. Arrange as a pair for asymmetry.

luxury.product: RH Belgian Linen Euro Sham set in ivory + accent toss in velvet
luxury.note: European hem linen for an effortlessly rumpled look. Add one velvet toss in a jewel tone.

premium.product: Pottery Barn faux fur + West Elm brushed cotton pillow set
premium.note: Mix two textures: one smooth cotton, one plush. Stick to 3 pillows for a curated look.

budget.product: IKEA KARITANA + Target Threshold faux fur pillows
budget.note: Inexpensive way to add color and texture. Use removable covers for easy seasonal swaps.

luxury.product: RH Versailles Arched Mirror or DWR Full Length Oval Mirror
luxury.note: A mirror opposite a window doubles natural light and visually expands the room.

premium.product: West Elm Arch Mirror or CB2 Sunburst Mirror
premium.note: Architectural shape that reads as art. Lean against the wall for an instant focal point.

budget.product: IKEA HOVET or IKEA STAVE floor mirror
budget.note: Large-scale presence at a fraction of the cost. The simple frame won't compete with artwork.

luxury.note: Large-format art and design books that signal taste. Stack of 3 in graduated sizes.

premium.product: Vox 'The Art of Living' + 'Cabins' by Gestalten
premium.note: Curated coffee table books with high production value. Focus on architecture or interiors.

budget.product: Secondhand Taschen + design magazines from independent publishers
budget.note: Mix hardcovers with a few current design magazines for a collected-over-time look.

shopping_guide.shopping_philosophy: For this living room, invest in the sofa and lighting (where quality matters most), save on side tables and accessories (where style can be found at any price). Budget tier recommendations are included for clients who want a staged approach.

product_summary.recommended_product: Crate & Barrel Lounge II — rolled-arm with skirt or exposed legs
Pottery Barn Turner accent chair — wingback or club chair
CB2 Tripod Floor Lamp in black or brass
Hudson Valley Ormand Picture Light in aged brass
Pottery Barn Belden or West Elm Sphere Table Lamp
West Elm Geo Trellis or CB2 Hand-Knotted Wool Rug
Crate & Barrel Liam Coffee Table or CB2 Plinth Table in oak
IKEA LÖVBACKEN or VITTSJÖ nesting tables
